product: Row filtering for managed ingestion connectors (Beta)
tagline: Lakeflow Connect now offers row filtering for managed ingestion connectors to improve performance and minimize data duplication.
what_it_does: Lakeflow Connect now offers row filtering for managed ingestion connectors to improve performance and minimize data duplication. Row filtering applies conditions similar to a SQL WHERE clause, allowing you to ingest only the data you need from your source systems. This feature is available for the Google Analytics, Salesforce, and ServiceNow connectors.
why_it_matters: Lakeflow Connect now offers row filtering for managed ingestion connectors to improve performance and minimize data duplication.
plain_summary: Lakeflow Connect now offers row filtering for managed ingestion connectors to improve performance and minimize data duplication. Row filtering applies conditions similar to a SQL WHERE clause, allowing you to ingest only the data you need from your source…

capabilities.title: You define a row filter
capabilities.desc: You define a row filter per source object (table, stream, or object) as a boolean expression over that object's columns.

capabilities.title: The filter is applied during
capabilities.desc: The filter is applied during the initial load and on every subsequent incremental update, so the target stays consistent with…

capabilities.title: Because filtering happens before transfer
capabilities.desc: Because filtering happens before transfer, it reduces data movement, storage, and downstream compute compared with ingesting…

prerequisites: A Lakeflow Connect ingestion pipeline on a supported connector.
Unity Catalog enabled for the destination catalog and schema.
Permission to edit the ingestion pipeline definition.
use_cases: Ingest only rows for a specific region, business unit, or active-record flag.
Exclude test or archived records from a SaaS system.
Reduce cost and duplication for very large source tables.
limitations: Filters reference only columns available on the source object; joins across objects are not supported in a filter.
Changing a filter after the initial load can require a full refresh of the affected object to remain consistent.
Non-deterministic source functions are not supported inside a filter.
key_takeaway: Lakeflow Connect now offers row filtering for managed ingestion connectors to improve performance and minimize data duplication.
